calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion, And Motion For A Temporary Stay Of Execution Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure Section 918; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support
Off Calendar Oct-11-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Joseph J. Poppen, Esq. of Bryan Cave Leighton Paisner, LLP, T:415.675.3470, for petitioner/moving party:
Majed Dakak, Esq. Kesselman Brantly Stockinger LLP, T:310.307.4559, for respondent/opposing party.

RULING: The matter was contested and argued. At the conclusion of arguments, the Court adopted the tentative ruling on PETITIONER TARA MATERIALS, INC.'s Motion For A Temporary Stay Of Execution Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure Section 918.

The court grants Tara Material Inc.'s motion for a temporary stay of execution, which shall expire "10 days beyond the last date on which a notice of appeal could be filed." (Code of Civ. Proc., 918(b). The court finds good cause for issuance of the stay given the amount of the judgment and difficulty in obtaining a bond of nearly $5,000,000. (Benator Dec., 3.) As a condition of this order, the court orders Tara not to transfer any assets outside the course of ordinary business during the stay's pendency.

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Entry Of Judgment And Award Of Prejudgment Interest
Off Calendar Oct-11-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Majed Dakak, Esq. Kesselman Brantly Stockinger LLP, T:310.307.4559, for respondent/moving party;
Joseph J. Poppen, Esq. of Bryan Cave Leighton Paisner, LLP, T:415.675.3470, for petitioner/opposing party.

RULING: The matter was contested and argued. At the conclusion of arguments, the Court adopted the tentative ruling on RESPONDENT ATI INDUSTRIES, INC.'s Motion For Entry Of Judgment And Award Of Prejudgment Interest, as follows:

ATI Industries, Inc.'s motion for entry of judgment and motion to recover post-arbitration award and prejudgment interest at a rate of 10% is granted.

In Britz, Inc. v. Alfa-Laval Food & Dairy Co. (1995) 34 Cal.App.4th 1085, the court held that a successful party to arbitration is entitled to post-award, pre-judgment interest under Civil Code section 3287, subdivision (a). The Britz court held that a successful party to an arbitration is entitled to "recover damages certain" within the meaning of section 3287, subdivision (a) as of the date the arbitrator renders his or her award. (Id. at 1106.) The court noted that "[t]he arbitration award itself result[s] in a new and fixed liability [citation]. Regardless of the individual elements that comprised that liability, respondents were entitled to payment of the fixed sum upon issuance of the award. [].... Although the interest was pre-'judicial judgment,' it was post-'contractual judgment.' Any result that denied respondents this post-award interest would punish them for using arbitration instead of the court system to resolve their dispute with appellants." (Id. at 1107; see also Tenzera, Inc. v. Osterman (2012) 205 Cal.App.4th 16, 21 ["A prevailing party in arbitration is entitled to prejudgment interest as of the date of the final award to entry of judgment"]; Pierotti v. Torian (2000) 81 Cal.App.4th 17, 26-28 [party that prevailed on petition to confirm arbitration award was entitled to post-award, pre-judgment interest as a matter of right].)

Cal. Civil Code, 3289(b) provides: "If a contract entered into after January 1, 1986, does not stipulate a legal rate of interest, the obligation shall bear interest at a rate of 10 percent per annum after a breach." Cal. Code of Civ. Proc., 1287.6 provides that "[a]n award that has not been confirmed or vacated has the same force and effect as a contract in writing between the parties to the arbitration." ATI is therefore entitled to prejudgment interest at a rate of 10%. It does not matter whether the underlying claims were equitable because under Britz, the liability became fixed and the individual elements that comprised the liability are no longer relevant.

Order and Judgment signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com
